sn2.8:1.2: Then Tāyana the deva’s son, formerly of another religious sect, in the far extreme of the night, his extreme radiance lighting up the entirety of Jeta’s Grove, went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, having bowed down to him, stood to one side.
sn2.8:1.3: As he was standing there, he recited these verses in the Blessed One’s presence:
sn2.8:2.3: Without abandoning sensual passions, a sage
sn2.8:3.1: If something’s to be done, then work at it firmly,
sn2.8:3.3: kicks up all the more dust.
sn2.8:3.4: It’s better to leave a misdeed undone.
sn2.8:4.3: Better that a good deed be done
sn2.8:4.4: that, when done, you don’t regret.
sn2.8:5.1: Just as sharp-bladed grass, if wrongly held,
sn2.8:5.2: wounds the very hand that holds it—
sn2.8:5.3: the contemplative life, if wrongly grasped,
sn2.8:7.1: That is what Tāyana the deva’s son said.
sn2.8:7.2: Having said it, he bowed down to the Blessed One and, circling him on the right, vanished right there.
sn2.8:8.1: Then, toward the end of the night, the Blessed One addressed the monks:
sn2.8:8.2: “Last night, monks, Tāyana the deva’s son, formerly of another religious sect, in the far extreme of the night, his extreme radiance lighting up the entirety of Jeta’s Grove, came to me and, on arrival, having bowed down to me, stood to one side.
sn2.8:8.3: As he was standing there, he recited these verses in my presence:
sn2.8:9.3: Without abandoning sensual passions, a sage
sn2.8:10.1: If something’s to be done, then work at it firmly,
sn2.8:10.3: kicks up all the more dust.
sn2.8:10.4: It’s better to leave a misdeed undone.
sn2.8:11.2: Better that a good deed be done
sn2.8:11.3: that, when done, you don’t regret.
sn2.8:11.4: Just as sharp-bladed grass, if wrongly held,
sn2.8:12.1: wounds the very hand that holds it—
sn2.8:12.2: the contemplative life, if wrongly grasped,
sn2.8:13.4: That is what Tāyana the deva’s son said.
sn2.8:14.1: Having said it, he bowed down to me and, circling me on the right, vanished right there.
sn2.8:14.5: Connected with the goal are Tāyana’s verses, monks, and basic to the holy life.”
